calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-11-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Set For Trial On Dec-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John J. Hughes of the law offices of John J. Hughes (415-928-4200) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Juliette B. McCullough of the law offices of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP (415-438-4600) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS HORNBLOWER FLEET, LLC and HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC DBA HORNBLOWER CRUISES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the first two causes of action for unseaworthiness and negligence (Jones Act) in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Debra Rees is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Paragraph 11 of the FAC alleges sufficient facts to support the assertion that defendants are equitably estopped from raising the 3-year statute of limitations. The allegations in paragraph 11 are closer to Atkins v. Union Pacific Railroad Co. (9th Cir. 1982) than Huseman v. Icicle Seafoods, Inc. (9th Cir. 2006) 471 F.3d 1116. Per Atkins, Ms. Rees has adequately alleged that, because of Jennifer Gray's email of August 2, 2016 stating that Ms. Gray will "reconnect" with Ms. Rees about "anything needed related to" Ms. Rees' September 20, 2013 injury, Ms. Rees was lulled into a false sense of security that she would be informed about "anything" that she "needed" to do to ensure that she could pursue her claims about the September 20, 2013 injury. After re-reading the initial complaint filed by Ms. Rees in light of Atkins, the court now believes that the demurrer to the original complaint probably should have been overruled. In all events, the FAC alleges sufficient facts - including the beefed-up allegations about Ms. Rees' understanding of Ms. Gray's August 2, 2016 email - that a reasonable trier of fact could find that defendants are equitably estopped from asserting the statute of limitations. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2017 Continued To Nov-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John J. Hughes of the law offices of John J. Hughes (415-928-4200) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Juliette B. McCullough of the law offices of Cox Wootton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP (415-438-4600) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS HORNBLOWER FLEET, LLC and HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, to the first and second causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Debra is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. The face of the complaint shows that, absent an applicable exception or tolling rule, the 3 year statute of limitations bars Ms. Rees' for unseaworthiness and negligence per the Jones Act. The injury occurred on September 20, 2013 and Ms. Rees filed this action on April 24, 2017. Federal maritime law preempts application of 10 CCR 2695.7 and Insurance Code 11583. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Rees is given leave to amend to allege facts showing that defendants should be estopped from relying on the statute of limitations. The complaint presently does not allege conduct showing that defendants actively prevented Ms. Rees from filing her claims or that they lulled Ms. Rees into a false sense of security. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No: 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
